title: Atomic verifier-only bootstrap repair candidate

intent_binding.endgame: authoritative local truth followed by a clean candidate, external authority continuity proof, final integration, and the first real non-fixture coding loop
intent_binding.functional_slice_first: structured authority plus initial v2 receipt to preflight, staged complete harness, exact status, and atomic promotion
intent_binding.minimal_governance: no vault, signer daemon, keychain adapter, recovery service, database, generic contract kernel, or broad decomposition program

algorithm_boundary_decision.decision: Require Ed25519 for structured v2 authority contracts and legacy raw verifier documents.
algorithm_boundary_decision.legacy_receipts: read-only verification remains supported; new canonical mutation rejects legacy v1 receipts
algorithm_boundary_decision.d078_compatibility: preserved because the current legacy D078 public JWK is already OKP/Ed25519 and MH_TRUTH_001 passes against the existing ledger

implemented: Extracted public authority validation and installation into lib/truth-authority-contract.js while retaining a verifier-only production surface.
Pinned structured and legacy public JWK validation to OKP Ed25519 with non-empty x and absent d.
Added an explicit 300-second maximum authority receipt lifetime in verifier policy.
Added read-only initial bootstrap preflight against the supplied in-memory structured authority document before any target write.

Preflight validates authority installation state, v2 receipt shape, capability, empty prior, proposal, repository identity, signer identity, signature, receipt window, initial snapshot semantics, and simulated one-event canonical history.
Changed init to materialize the complete harness in a temporary sibling root and promote it only after successful staged append and exact status generation.
Added rollback of an existing harness if final directory promotion fails.
Added a root bootstrap lock plus a second under-lock preflight so concurrent valid initialization installs exactly one authority.
Added an actionable legacy-authority migration error and retained an exact structured-authority mismatch error.
Split bootstrap adversarial tests from canonical mutation tests and returned all four reported quality deltas to policy compliance without refreshing the baseline.

audit_finding_disposition.finding: Exact init write sequence before receipt validation
audit_finding_disposition.evidence: preflightInitialCanonicalReceipt executes before promoteBootstrap; ensureHarness, all starter writes, authority installation, event append, and status generation occur only inside the staged root after preflight
truth-authority-contract validation requires kty=OKP, crv=Ed25519, non-empty x, and absent d before crypto.createPublicKey

audit_finding_disposition.finding: Legacy algorithm boundary could break D078 or remain generic
audit_finding_disposition.evidence: legacy raw authority documents use the same Ed25519 validator; an isolated legacy Ed25519 history test passes, legacy RSA is rejected, and the current D078 MH_TRUTH_001 check passes
MAX_RECEIPT_TTL_SECONDS equals 300 and verifier rejection is covered by a 301-second byte-identical bootstrap test

audit_finding_disposition.finding: Legacy EEXIST path returned a generic/internal error
audit_finding_disposition.evidence: existing raw legacy authority returns TRUTH_AUTHORITY_LEGACY_KEY_PRESENT through MH_TRUTH_AUTHORITY with a reviewed migration instruction and no target mutation

audit_finding_disposition.finding: CANDIDATE-S001 did not explicitly require structured v2 authority state
audit_finding_disposition.evidence: candidate exit condition now requires a tracked structured v2 authority contract using the current Ed25519 key and explicitly rejects a raw legacy JWK

focused_truth_and_bootstrap.runtime: connector Node 18.19.1 used for focused logic verification only
focused_truth_and_bootstrap.command: node --test tests/cli-truth-bootstrap.test.js tests/cli-truth.test.js tests/truth-reconciler.test.js tests/cli-ready-truth.test.js tests/context-truth-pregate.test.js

supported_runtime_complete.execution: all 121 test files executed in deterministic runner-equivalent parallel and serial batches because the monolithic connector response returned transport 502 errors

adversarial_proofs: Invalid signature, expired, 301-second lifetime, wrong repository, wrong prior, non-snapshot, tampered proposal, and RSA authority bootstrap attempts leave the target tree byte-identical.
A failed hostile bootstrap leaves no .meta-harness authority claim and a subsequent legitimate authority initializes successfully.
Two concurrent valid bootstrap attempts produce exactly one accepted authority installation and one rejected invocation, with one initial event and no residual lock.
An existing raw legacy authority produces an actionable migration-required authority error without mutation.
Legacy Ed25519 signed history remains readable while legacy RSA authority is rejected.
Existing replay, cross-repository binding, concurrent single-receipt append, exact projection, append-only reconciliation, and non-zero truth-block behavior remain green.
Production and dry-run package scans find no key generation, private-key loading, signing API, private-key path, or test signer.

remaining_gates.proof: Independently reproduce the bootstrap matrix, D078 legacy truth verification, supported-runtime suite, quality result, package scan, and verifier-only source scan.
Reconcile upstream and create one named clean candidate containing S-001R3 and a tracked structured v2 authority contract, not a raw legacy JWK.
Use the matching external private capability once in the clean candidate checkout without exposing private material.

stop_rule: Do not claim S-001R3 accepted, change canonical state, isolate or commit a candidate, reconcile upstream, run G-001, push, merge, release-package, or begin S-006M before independent acceptance.
known_residual_risk: The checkout remains dirty and one commit behind origin/main; this repair is not check-outable until CANDIDATE-S001.
External private-capability continuity remains unproven until G-001.
A filesystem or Git writer can still remove a signed ledger tail; custody remains an integration concern rather than justification for a new database.
